prompt: Design a non-inverting op-amp amplifier with gain of ~11x for a low-frequency audio/sensor signal, single 5V supply, AC-coupled input and output.

placement_constraints.reason: supply decoupling cap must be close to op-amp VCC pin
keep feedback network short to minimize noise pickup

placement_rules_text: Keep the feedback network (R1, R2) tight to the op-amp inverting input to minimize stray capacitance and noise pickup. Place the decoupling cap C3 as close as possible to the op-amp supply pin. Keep the input coupling path (C1, R3) away from the output trace (AMP_OUT, C2) to avoid feedback coupling. The Vref divider (R4, R5, C4) can be placed anywhere convenient but should stay on one side to keep its trace short.

test_points.name: TP_OUT
test_points.purpose: probe amplifier output before AC coupling

routing_hints: Route FEEDBACK_NODE as a short, direct trace with minimal vias.
Keep GND as a solid pour where possible; avoid splitting the ground plane under U1.
Route VIN_COUPLED away from VCC and digital traces if any exist on the board.
AMP_OUT to C2 should be routed before fanning out to any other test/probe point.

review_flags.message: R3 sets input bias impedance; verify it does not dominate source impedance for the intended signal source.
OPA2340 is a dual op-amp package (SOIC-8) but only one channel is used here; confirm the unused half is not left floating if using the real dual-channel part.

test_plan.name: Power-up DC bias
test_plan.rationale: single-supply op-amp needs a stable rail before any AC test is meaningful
test_plan.fail_action: check supply source and C3 solder joint before proceeding

test_plan.name: VREF bias point
test_plan.rationale: sets the DC operating point for the whole amplifier; everything downstream depends on this
test_plan.fail_action: check R4/R5 values and C4 polarity/leakage

test_plan.name: Output DC operating point
test_plan.condition: VIN grounded through R3, no AC signal
test_plan.rationale: should track VREF; large deviation indicates input offset voltage amplified by gain
test_plan.fail_action: compare U1 Vos_max x gain(11) against observed offset

test_plan.name: Gain verification
test_plan.rationale: this is the core spec of the circuit; matches performance_specs.gain_v_v

test_plan.name: Bandwidth (-3dB point)
test_plan.rationale: OPA2340 GBW must exceed target_bw x gain(11) with margin
test_plan.fail_action: if bandwidth is low, gain (R2/R1) is too aggressive for this op-amp

test_plan.name: Low-frequency AC-coupling corner
test_plan.rationale: confirms input coupling doesn't clip low-frequency content of interest
test_plan.fail_action: adjust C1/R3 if real signal has content below this corner

test_plan.name: Output swing / clipping
test_plan.condition: increase input amplitude until visible clipping

expected.note: clips near rails minus op-amp headroom

test_plan.rationale: at gain 11, even small inputs approach full swing; defines max usable input range
test_plan.fail_action: reduce input amplitude or lower gain if usable range is too narrow

test_plan.name: THD at target output level
test_plan.rationale: matches performance_specs.target_thd_percent at moderate output levels
test_plan.fail_action: check output loading and supply decoupling if THD is high

test_plan.name: Output noise floor
test_plan.condition: no input signal, input terminated with expected source impedance

expected.note: consistent with OPA2340 input noise spec x gain(11) x sqrt(bandwidth)

test_plan.rationale: elevated noise usually indicates poor decoupling or ground loop issues
test_plan.fail_action: verify C3 placement and grounding per placement_constraints

test_plan.name: Supply rejection sanity check

expected.note: attenuated per op-amp PSRR spec; should not feed through directly

test_plan.rationale: C3 (100nF) should be absorbing high-frequency supply noise
test_plan.fail_action: confirm C3 is within 3mm of U1 VCC pin per placement_constraints

expected.note: C3 within 3mm of U1 VCC; R1/R2 loop short; VIN_COUPLED not routed parallel to AMP_OUT
